title: Level 350
wikitext:
{{Background|file=lefttorot.webp}} {{Quote|What anyone must acknowledge is that nothing is permanent. Nothing will stay forever. Time catches up with everything at some point. The sadness is triggered when they do not know there is no such thing as "permanent," quite when they have to learn the hard way.}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 350: "Left To Rot"}}
= '''Level 350: "Left To Rot"''' =
{{Image Banner|image=lefttorot.webp|caption=''Do you accept this impermanence?''}} {{Alt Class|no=350|ext=2|env=3|ent=0}} '''Level 350''' is designated as the 351st level of the Backrooms, '''slowly decaying.'''
'''Description'''
{{Blurimage|caption=An image of the courtyards in '''Level 350'''.|file=lefttorot.webp|hovertext=The place is full of plants.}} {{Blurimage|caption=An image of a hallway in '''Level 350'''.|file=Decayinghalls.webp|hovertext=The place is full of dangers.}}
In appearance and structure, '''Level 350''' is a hotel in the middle of an ocean. It, vernacularly named "Left To Rot," has gradually decayed since 2013, although the causality is unexplored. The level's hotel is roughly 2,500 miles squared, harboring seven floors. The hotel's external appearance reminisces a minimalist architectural style, featuring peach-colored painted concrete. There exists a sum of four courtyard-like areas across the hotel's structure. These courtyards comprise turf flooring with dispersed potted plants; the courtyards partially go into the structure and configuration, creating an overhang. Above these are balconies accessible from the third floor; these balconies have wooden and tiled flooring and overlook the sea that encircles the hotel. These balconies enclose meager décor entangling lampposts, chairs & tables, and potted plants. All four of the courtyards and porches are matching in design and layout. The hotel's inner appearance is in sweeping neglect: broken windows, furniture out-of-place, plant pots fall, torn curtains, peeling wallpaper, and a pervasive dust layer enfolding every surface. Many perceive the rooms as forsaken in terms of their physical vacuity and the deficiency of atmosphere. The corridors retain spaces usually holding doors, although some harbor wreckage of the same. The corridors usually embody approximately 150-100 feet in length (50-30 meters), 10-15 feet in width (3-5 meters), and 16 feet in height (5 meters), although the value can fluctuate, assuming the hotel's deviations. Additionally, the hotel's inner is dwelling to remains of numerous human results. Moreover, due to several phenomena, weather, and more, the plants have adapted to the level's environment and evolved into exotic or mutated forms; however, the level is also home to known plants from Earth, including Decaying Blossoms, Coralvines, Lamentia Ferns, Shroud Fungi, and more. The ocean contrasts with the hotel's disrepair; it is serene, placid, and boundless. The water is saline, resembling [[The Frontrooms]]. The sea is indemnity to swim, although imprudent wanders can lead to perdition, conducting to an unsafe level; however, abiding close to the sea is a mindful option due to the dearth of dangers and aggravations that might guide to demise. The sea is house to sea life and, predominantly, vegetation due to the exponential growth. Consuming anything from the sea is not a good option. Once the ocean is near the hotel, it leads the sea to harbor dust, concrete, windows, and more. Furthermore, the vegetation in the sea comprises things from the hotel, including those. Although the ocean is boundless, it is noteworthy to refrain from ingesting it. In dangers and hazards, '''Level 350''', although replete in wonders and classified as Class 1, is furthermore concealed in riskiness. For being a level girded in plants, herbs, trees, shrubs, vines, and additional botanic, it is considerable the presence of intoxications by plants. In environments rich in botanical diversity, there are odds regarding potential risks when consuming, breathing, or further dealings with plants. Amidst the hotel's structure, as heretofore said, areas holding glasses, planks, furniture out-of-place, and even wrecked parts can be harmful and deleterious concerning the likelihood of harm to wanderers; it is, tetanus infection, minor injuries to fractures or dislocation, claustrophobia, unsafe pathways, fallen debris, potential exposure to biohazards, electrical hazards, chemical exposure, instability, inadequate lighting, the risk of encountering sharp objects, slipping hazards, tripping risks, exposure to airborne contaminants, damaged infrastructure due to human works, and the menace of falling objects from erratic surroundings; thus, it is diplomatic to convey appropriate clothing against sharp objects, sturdy footwear for navigating uneven surfaces, and perhaps a portable light source for areas with inadequate lighting. {{Blurimage|caption=Level 350's Currents|file=Level 350 Currents.webp|hovertext=The currents are highly changed due to the sea's size.}} In phenomena and climate, '''Level 350''' harbors similarities to [[The Frontrooms]], particularly in its weather patterns. The ocean directly influences the weather and temperature; given the vast and boundless ocean's size, the ocean currents evolve decisive to the level. Weather conditions can swerve; nevertheless, given that the sea's size is limitless, it would suggest that ocean currents broadcasting around the level are extensive and unending. It could guide to more stable and consistent weather patterns within the level; however, the water's circulation conveys a constant debris influx or pollutants from an unidentified place. Additionally, without apparently natural barriers, weather patterns could become more erratic. Therefore, warm currents may raise local temperatures, while cold currents could have a cooling effect; ocean currents influence the movement of air above the sea, and extreme temperature fluctuations, seasonal variations, storms, and ecological impact can be a direct consequence of the currents and the sea's measure. Occasionally, the ocean can be considered a dead sea owing to its size, conditions, phenomena, and currents. It pushes the salt way more concentrate to the coast, near the hotel. Consequently, '''Level 350''' is a valuable salt resource for [[M.E.G]], a resource thought deprecated to the backrooms. The ocean currents within '''Level 350''' averages around 1.5 (~0.77 m/s and ~2.77km/h) to 2.5 knots (~1.28m/s and ~4.63km/h). However, areas influenced by underwater terrain or other phenomena may exhibit currents with velocities ranging from 2.5 to 4 knots (~2.05m/s to ~7.40km/h), although some can abruptly shift to 10 knots (~5.144m/s to ~18.52km/h). Given the sea's massive size, currents span hundreds or sometimes even thousands of kilometers, driving winds much more prolonged. Along with the speed, it can push water infiltration, debris transport, structure damage, and more. The water infiltration, however, is automatically consumed by the plants. Since the level does not contain a rotation, [[M.E.G.]] scientists suddenly suggested that, due to the ocean being indefinite, it would exert a gravitational force that intricately assembles its winds; however, despite the sea being supposedly the gravity's primary point, it accomplishes no explanations concerning the discrepancy between the acceleration's modifications; accordingly, scientists proposed a theory named "Dimensional Anomalies." It explains that other ocean levels would coexist with '''Level 350''', shipping currently untapped energy like a ship. This phenomenon might explain the sudden cessation of winds and currents in other ocean levels. Although this theory holds numerous believers throughout the backrooms, it is nevertheless unfathomable the reason for all of this. In contrast, '''Level 350''' is thought secure by many. Although the level does not shelter entities or deadly things, it is crucial to have the proper resources and, principally, go in groups.
'''Entities'''
'''Level 350''' is devoid of of any entities. Its lone inhabitants are the overgrowth.
'''Bases, Communities, & Outposts'''
'''Level 350''' had not established any bases, communities, and outposts that weathered due to the level's past. The level is the abode of their remains.
'''History'''
'''Level 350''' stood foremost uncovered in 1988 by [[M.E.G.]]. They interpreted the level ideal for all categories of settlements, given the valuable resources like [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and the absence of any dangerous entity. Thereon, considerable groups arranged shop within the level; most existed there to abuse the level's bountiful resources. As everyone surmised that '''Level 350''' was one of the safest levels, besides the ranks of levels embracing [[Level 9.6]], and [[Level 154]], it suddenly began to decay; weeds came through cracks, gaps, and fissures in the concrete; plants developed beyond their pottery confines. During a phase in 2013, all supplies, reserves, and stock coexisting in the level vanished. Owing to the now excessive shortage of supplies, all the companies fled the level. Subsequently, they departed the peach-colored that were once-paradise to rot in stillness in the middle of an eternal and inexhaustible ocean. Nowadays, '''Level 350''' is nevertheless uninhabited by any life, excluding plants, and looking ahead, it is unspecified what will happen to the level once the hotel has shriveled. Folks foreknow that day as the belatedly 2100s. Presently, the level is serene, with solely the sounds of splashing waves; additionally, the level is now entire with artifacts from past wanderers and groups once living in '''Level 350'''.
'''Entrances and Exits'''
=== Entrances ===
* No-clipping into one of the modern sections of [[Level 5]] usually lead to this level. * No-clipping through a beige concrete wall in [[Level 427]] leads one here. === Exits ===
* Finding a hallway with flowers on the floor will lead to [[Level 70]]. * Entering a door made from scrap wood or metal can lead to [[Level 375]]. ---- {{Redirect|Prev=349|Next=351}} ----
'''What we took advantage of.'''
= '''Level 350: "Hotel Amidst the Ocean"''' = {{Image Banner|image=Beforedecay.png|caption=''Visit sometime!''}} {{Alt Class|no=350|ext=0|env=0|ent=0}} '''Level 350''' is the 351st level of the Backrooms.
'''Description'''
{{Blurimage|caption=A image of one of the hallways in '''Level 350'''.|file=Beforedecay.png|hovertext=You can rest now.}}
In appearance and structure, '''Level 350''', vernacularly named "Hotel Amidst the Ocean," is an extensive, square-shaped hotel surrounded by an inexhaustible ocean. The hotel is a seven-story building. The level is known for its diverse plant life and safety. The level's hotel is roughly 2,500 miles squared, harboring seven floors. The hotel's external appearance reminisces a minimalist architectural style, featuring peach-colored painted concrete. There exists a sum of four courtyard-like areas across the hotel's structure. These courtyards comprise turf flooring with dispersed potted plants; the courtyards partially go into the structure and configuration, creating an overhang. Above these are balconies accessible from the third floor; these balconies have wooden and tiled flooring and overlook the sea that encircles the hotel. These balconies have many decorations, such as chairs & tables, umbrellas, lampposts, and LED lighting that can change colors occasionally. The hotel's inner appearance is as clean and purely state; furthermore, '''Level 350''' is the residence of all the settlements. The hallways' appearance alters highly, notwithstanding some superficial features such as carpeted floors and smooth ceilings. The corridors usually embody roughly 150-100 feet in length (50-30 meters), 10-15 feet in width (3-5 meters), and 16 feet in height (5 meters), although the value can fluctuate, assuming the hotel's deviations. The rooms may also contrast, though they are multiple distinctive rectangular shapes; nonetheless, the placement of walls can vary, including the furniture's placement. All the furniture in the hotel rooms operates agreeably, revealing that one could take a good shower or even sleep. [[M.E.G.]] newly discovered that the water from the shower arrives from the ocean undergoing an unbeknownst treatment until the pipes exit the level. The level is deemed a tourist spot in the backrooms owing to the diverse botanic, cleanliness, beauty, safety, community, resources, and more. Many visit the place to engage in enjoyment and uneventful activities. These pursuits encompass various actions from a wanderer's past experiences on earth, such as gardening, beachside relaxation, socializing, forming connections, working as a security guard, engaging in trade, swimming, and other similar pastimes. It points out that the level is likewise a safe harbor, blending a hotel that holds hotel rooms, bedrooms, corridors, spots, and other locations uncontroversial. The community is safe and trustworthy, helping and sharing resources with everyone. The place is supervised by [[M.E.G.]] guards, preserving the protection of the guests. These details complete the level of a place where numerous wanderers expect to go, devoid of any harmful beings besides the pretentiousness of the wanderers. The ocean matches with the hotel's beauty; it is serene, placid, and boundless. The water is saline, resembling [[The Frontrooms]]. The sea is indemnity to swim, although imprudent wanders can lead to perdition, conducting to an unsafe level; however, abiding close to the sea is a mindful option due to the dearth of dangers and aggravations that might guide to demise. The sea is house to sea life and, predominantly, vegetation due to the exponential growth. Consuming anything from the sea is not a good option, although there are water treatment procedures you can take to make it safe to drink. {{Blurimage|caption=Level 350's Currents|file=Level 350 Currents.webp|hovertext=The currents are highly changed due to the sea's size.}} In phenomena and climate, '''Level 350''' harbors similarities to [[The Frontrooms]], particularly in its weather patterns. The ocean directly influences the weather and temperature; given the vast and boundless ocean's size, the ocean currents evolve decisive to the level. Weather conditions can swerve; nevertheless, given that the sea's size is limitless, it would suggest that ocean currents broadcasting around the level are extensive and unending. Additionally, without apparently natural barriers, weather patterns could become more erratic. Therefore, warm currents may raise local temperatures, while cold currents could have a cooling effect; ocean currents influence the movement of air above the sea, and extreme temperature fluctuations, seasonal variations, storms, and ecological impact can be a direct consequence of the currents and the sea's measure. Occasionally, the ocean can be considered a dead sea owing to its size, conditions, phenomena, and currents. It pushes the salt way more concentrate to the coast, near the hotel. Consequently, '''Level 350''' is a valuable salt resource for [[M.E.G]], a resource thought deprecated to the backrooms. The ocean currents within '''Level 350''' averages around 1.5 (~0.77 m/s and ~2.77km/h) to 2.5 knots (~1.28m/s and ~4.63km/h). However, areas influenced by underwater terrain or other phenomena may exhibit currents with velocities ranging from 2.5 to 4 knots (~2.05m/s to ~7.40km/h), although some can abruptly shift to 10 knots (~5.144m/s to ~18.52km/h). Given the sea's massive size, currents span hundreds or sometimes even thousands of kilometers, driving winds much more prolonged. The water infiltration, however, is automatically consumed by the plants. It explains that other ocean levels would coexist with '''Level 350''', shipping currently untapped energy like a ship. This phenomenon might explain the sudden cessation of winds and currents in other ocean levels. '''Entities'''
'''Level 350''' is devoid of any entities.
'''Bases, Communities, and Outposts'''
Due to the abundance of resources in the level, many of these settlements have arisen in the hotel. === [[M.E.G.]] (Major Explorer Group) Outposts "Hotel Crew" === * Consists of fifty-five (55) members, they are stationed here mainly to collect resources. * Provide assistance to some of the other groups within '''Level 350'''. * Non-hostile and willing to trade and accept new members. === [[Backroom Colonists|Backrooms Colonists]] Base Peach === * Has thirty-nine (39) members, they are mainly here to trade resources. * Willing to trade to any group of trust. * Might accept new members. === The Stragglers === * Have around fifteen (15) members. It is unknown what they do. * They're the furthest group from anyone. * They are hostile only if provoked. * It is assumed they're only here to take resources. === [[Entity Research Committee|E.R.C.]] Base "Summer Vacation" === * Only has eleven (11) members. They study the plant life within the level. * Recruits new members occasionally. * Heavily armed but friendly. === The Residents of Level 350 === * Total population of '''Level 350''' that aren't associated with any group, currently at four-hundred-fifty (450). * Most are here to live in the many rooms across the hotel.
'''Entrances and Exits'''
=== Entrances ===
* No-clipping into one of the modern sections of [[Level 5]] usually lead to this level. * Noclipping through a beige concrete wall in [[Level 427]] leads one here. === Exits ===
* Finding a hallway with flowers on the floor will lead to [[Level 70]]. ---- {{Collapse|num=999|initial='''Click for Author Information'''|final='''Close Author Information''' |body= {{Audio|file=ForgottenN7ICX.mp3|volume=1}} *'''Written by''': [[User:Arkansas Animations|Arkansas Animations]] and [[User:ReDcosmic|Red]]. *'''Additional Contributors''' (Wikitext Tweaks):[[User:Blackexeishere|The Azure Deep]] *'''Song''': [[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=edTlQUuQGno]]}}
